items.title: Meta will record employees’ keystrokes and use it to train its AI models
items.summary: Meta says that it has a new internal tool that is converting mouse movements and button clicks into data that can train its AI models.

items.title: Unauthorized group has gained access to Anthropic’s exclusive cyber tool Mythos, report claims
items.summary: Anthropic told TechCrunch it is investigating the claims, but maintains that there is no evidence that its systems have been impacted.

items.title: AI research lab NeoCognition lands $40M seed to build agents that learn like humans

items.title: ChatGPT’s new Images 2.0 model is surprisingly good at generating text
items.summary: ChatGPT Images 2.0, the newest image-generation model from OpenAI, shows just how much AI capabilities have evolved over the last few years.

items.title: Sam Altman throws shade at Anthropic’s cyber model, Mythos: ‘fear-based marketing’
items.summary: This week, during a podcast appearance, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman called out his competitor's new cybersecurity model, noting that the company was using fear to make its product sound more impressive than it actually is.

items.title: Clarifai deletes 3 million photos that OkCupid provided to train facial recognition AI, report says
items.summary: The photo deletion comes after an FTC settlement with Clarifai. The company had asked OkCupid — whose executives had invested in Clarifai — to share data in 2014, according to court documents.

items.title: AI Dungeon maker Latitude unveils Voyage, a platform for creating AI-powered RPGs
items.summary: Latitude's new AI-native platform, Voyage, aims to help gamers create their very own role-playing game.

items.title: Bond, a new social media platform, wants to use AI to help you kick your doomscrolling habit
items.summary: Bond wants you to get off the couch and get back into the real world, its creator says. The new platform's AI system is designed to motivate users to do things away from the app.

items.title: YouTube expands its AI likeness detection technology to celebrities
items.summary: YouTube is expanding its AI likeness detection tool to celebrities, giving talent and their reps a way to find and remove deepfakes.
